title: Elderly woman dies of snakebite in Idukki; state's toll rises to 6 in a week
content: Thodupuzha: A 75-year-old woman died after being bitten by a snake at Thodupuzha, Idukki district, on Sunday. The deceased has been identified as Vishalakshi, a native of Kanjhar in Kudayathoor near Thodupuzha. The incident occurred around noon near her residence. Vishalakshi, who lived alone, was reportedly bitten by the snake while she had stepped out into the yard to collect mangoes. Despite being bitten, she managed to reach a nearby house and inform the residents about the incident. She was immediately rushed to a hospital in Thodupuzha by the neighbours, but doctors were unable to save her life. Vishalakshi's death raises the fatalities from snake bites in the state to 6 in the past week. Earlier on Sunday, a 70-year-old woman also died of snakebite at Muthukuda in Pattuvam in Kannur. Doctors identified it as a case of snakebite based on her symptoms, and tests confirmed envenomation, though no visible bite marks were found. The other victims include Indira (65) and Saleena (42) from Alappuzha, Dikshal (8) from Thiruvananthapuram, and Aljo (8) from Thrissur. The Health Department has urged the public to seek immediate medical assistance in case of snakebite by dialling 108. Health Minister Veena George said the Kanivu 108 ambulance service ensures timely transport to hospitals equipped with anti-snake venom, with trained personnel providing first aid and continuous monitoring during transit.
